title: Notebook Course Bundle Contract
intent: Define the minimum bundle that an ingestion-and-review pipeline should produce for a course or Notebook cluster, rather than stopping at an internal draft pack.

mode_position.note: The Notebook bundle is the higher-content output mode of the same stack, not a separate ad hoc project. Public deployment is performed through the SciSiteForge artifact exporter, not by publishing private staging state.

required_outputs.purpose: Present the learner-facing Notebook surface, cluster navigation, and machine-readable path entry points.
Expose the reviewed learner-facing concept explanation.
Expose reviewed reasoning records, source slots, tool links, and prompt seeds for downstream consumers.
Define the recommended instructional order, assumptions, and concept transitions.
Expose the currently discoverable Notebook bundles and their export artifacts without assuming a single current path.
Provide mentorship-oriented session goals, openings, evidence focus, and transition cues.
Guide scaffold-record ranking without hard-coding source- or topic-specific rules into Didactopus.
Expose reviewed claims, examples, questions, and coverage probes derived from staged source material.

recommended_outputs.purpose: Provide cross-concept evidence, history, or interpretation pages that extend beyond single concepts.
Expose reviewed source trails or explicit pending source slots.

recommended_outputs.path_pattern: embedded in concept scaffold or concept page
recommended_outputs.purpose: Connect the Notebook to packs, apps, guides, and literature tools.
Preserve exercise/review-problem prompts as reviewed questions or coverage probes that test whether the Notebook can support the source's expected learner tasks.
Preserve textbook code or pseudocode as reviewed algorithm descriptions or JavaScript implementations with provenance, model assumptions, and expected-output checks.
Preserve equations, theorem-like statements, derivations, notation mappings, assumptions, parameter meanings, and mathematical coverage gaps.

private_outputs: staging claims, examples, questions, and source trails
prompt traces, model logs, and generation batches
unresolved review queues and source-slot analysis
public_release_boundary.source_use_rule: Public Notebook output should expose reviewed summaries, paraphrases, citations, and short attributed quotations where justified; local source locators and raw extracted source material remain private.

bundle_discovery_note: Bundle identifiers should be discoverable from /notebook/learning-paths/ by finding <bundle-id>.json files with matching .didactopus.json and .selection-policy.json companions.
